verdict_reasoning: BAX (BCL2-associated X protein) is a pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family member that resides predominantly as a soluble monomer in the cytosol of healthy cells. Upon apoptotic stimulation it translocates to the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM), undergoes conformational change and oligomerization, and forms pores that permeabilize the MOM to release cytochrome c. All membrane activity is directed at the cytoplasm-facing MOM; BAX has no signal peptide, no GPI anchor, no extracellular domain, and no documented stable pool at the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Therapeutic programs (BH3-mimetics, direct BAX activators, small molecules) target the cytosolic or MOM-resident intracellular pool. No evidence exists for extracellular-face accessibility by any modality.
